Mission Mars: Interactive exhibit focusing on the future of lunar and Martian exploration.
Saturn V Rocket: One of only three remaining Saturn V rockets on display at Rocket Park.
The Moonwalkers: An immersive show narrated by Tom Hanks in the Space Center Theater.
attractions.tips: Book tickets online in advance to save on admission. Arrive early to secure your spot on the popular NASA Tram Tours, as they can sell out quickly. Download the Space Center Houston app for an interactive map and daily schedule (Space Center Houston)
attractions.nearby: Lone Star Flight Museum, Nassau Bay Peninsula Wildlife Park, Clear Lake Park, Kemah Boardwalk (nearby region) (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: The Cynthia Woods Mitchell Pavilion presented by Huntsman (Official Website)
attractions.description: The Cynthia Woods Mitchell Pavilion is a world-class outdoor amphitheater that serves as a center for the performing arts and contemporary entertainment. It hosts a diverse lineup including the Houston Symphony, ballet, opera, and major touring artists in a lush forest setting (Official Website).
attractions.address: 2005 Lake Robbins Dr, The Woodlands, TX 77380, US (Ticketmaster)
attractions.region: Greater North Houston / The Woodlands (Visit Shenandoah)
attractions.hours: Box Office: Mon-Fri 10 AM - 5 PM. Event Days: Mon-Sat 10 AM - Intermission; Sun 12 PM - Intermission (Official Website).
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Box Office); Event days include Saturday and Sunday (Official Website).
attractions.admission: Varies by event. Performing arts events (Houston Symphony, etc.) are often free in the mezzanine and lawn. Contemporary concerts have tiered pricing (e.g., Pit: $249.50, Reserved: $59.50-$199.50, Lawn: $49.50). Children 5 and under are free on the lawn for most contemporary shows (Official Website, Ticketmaster).
highlights: Consistently ranked as one of the Top 5 Outdoor Amphitheaters in the World by Pollstar magazine (Visit The Woodlands).
Summer home for the Houston Symphony and frequent host to the Houston Ballet and Houston Grand Opera (Wikipedia).
Features a capacity of 16,500, with 6,500 covered seats and a spacious lawn for 10,000 (Wikipedia).
Operated as a 501(c)(3) nonprofit dedicated to making the arts accessible (Official Website).
Surrounded by lush forests, providing a unique open-air concert experience (Woodlands Online).
attractions.tips: The venue is cashless for all transactions including concessions and parking. Clear bag policy (12x12 or smaller) is strictly enforced. Food is permitted in a one-gallon plastic bag at contemporary events, with fewer restrictions for performing arts shows (Official Website).
attractions.nearby: The Woodlands Town Center, Town Green Park, The Woodlands Waterway, and various shopping and dining options (Tripadvisor, Woodlands Online).

attractions.name: No Label Brewing Company
attractions.description: No Label Brewing Co. is a family-owned craft brewery located in the heart of Old Katy, Texas, operating out of a uniquely repurposed 1940s rice silo. Established in 2010, the brewery has become a local staple known for its award-winning ales and lagers, such as the Perpetual Peace Wee Heavy and El Jefe Hefeweizen. The taproom offers a welcoming, family-and-dog-friendly atmosphere with a spacious outdoor area, daily food trucks, and regular community events. (No Label Brewing Co., Visit Houston)
attractions.hours: Monday - Thursday: 3:00 PM - 10:00 PM; Friday - Saturday: 12:00 PM - 12:00 AM; Sunday: 12:00 PM - 10:00 PM (No Label Brewing Co.)
attractions.admission: Free (Visit Houston)
highlights: Award-winning craft beers including Perpetual Peace (GABF Gold Medalist) (No Label Brewing Co.)
Located in a historic repurposed rice silo (Visit Houston)
Family and dog-friendly environment with a large outdoor seating area (Tripadvisor)
Daily rotation of local food trucks (No Label Brewing Co.)
Weekly community events such as Bingo, the No Label Running Club, and live music (No Label Brewing Co.)
Highly rated destination in the heart of Old Katy (Tripadvisor)
attractions.tips: Outside food is not permitted, but a different food truck is available every day to provide fresh meals. Visitors should check the Taproom Calendar for special events and deals, such as the "Second beer FREE for Teachers" on Thursdays. The brewery is very family-friendly, making it a great weekend spot for parents and pet owners alike. (No Label Brewing Co., Tripadvisor)
attractions.nearby: Katy Mills Mall, Mary Jo Peckham Park, Katy Heritage Museum, MKT Railroad Museum, Typhoon Texas Waterpark (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: 1940 Air Terminal Museum
attractions.description: Housed in Houston’s original 1940 art deco air terminal at William P. Hobby Airport, this museum preserves the heritage of civil aviation and the city's role in flight history (1940 Air Terminal Museum). Visitors can explore vintage aircraft, uniforms, and memorabilia while enjoying a front-row view of active runways (Chron). Note: The museum ceased operations on March 2, 2026, due to financial challenges (Wikipedia, CultureMap Houston).
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Wednesday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Thursday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Friday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Saturday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Sunday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM (1940 Air Terminal Museum)
attractions.admission: Adults: $10.00; Children: $5.00; Children 2 and under: Free; Museum members, US Military, Police and Firefighters: Free; Wings & Wheels / Open Ramp Days: $12 Adults, $6 Kids (1940 Air Terminal Museum, Houston On The Cheap)
highlights: Housed in a historic Art Deco terminal building listed on the National Register of Historic Places (Wikipedia)
Observation balcony providing unobstructed views of aircraft in motion at Hobby Airport (1940 Air Terminal Museum)
Extensive collection of vintage airline uniforms, memorabilia, and artifacts from the 'Golden Age' of flight (Yelp)
Quarterly 'Wings & Wheels' events and monthly 'Open Ramp Days' featuring static aircraft displays (1940 Air Terminal Museum)
Interactive exhibits including flight simulators located in the hangar (Yelp)
attractions.tips: Visit during monthly 'Open Ramp Days' for access to areas typically closed to the public and for the best plane spotting opportunities (1940 Air Terminal Museum). Be aware that the museum recently announced a suspension of operations, so check for current status before planning a visit (KHOU). The observation deck is highly recommended for photography enthusiasts and families (GetYourGuide).
attractions.nearby: William P. Hobby Airport, Almeda Mall, Pasadena Little Theatre, Gus Wortham Golf Course (Expedia)

attractions.name: Alley Theatre
attractions.description: The Alley Theatre is one of the largest professional nonprofit theatres in the United States and a Tony Award-winning cultural landmark in Houston's Theater District. Founded in 1947, it is renowned for its full-time Resident Acting Company and a diverse repertoire that includes world premieres, reimagined classics, and innovative new works. The theatre operates out of a striking Brutalist-style building featuring two distinct performance spaces: the 774-seat Hubbard Theatre and the more intimate 296-seat Neuhaus Theatre Alley Theatre.
attractions.address: 615 Texas Avenue, Houston, TX 77002 Alley Theatre
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 12:00 PM - 6:00 PM (Box Office hours); performance times vary Tripadvisor
attractions.days_open: Daily (Box Office hours); performance days vary Tripadvisor
attractions.admission: Prices vary by performance; Student/Educator: $17-$29, Military/Senior: $29-$35, Playwrights Welcome: Free (limited availability) Houston On The Cheap
highlights: Recipient of the Tony Award for Outstanding Regional Theatre Alley Theatre
Home to a rare full-time Resident Acting Company Alley Theatre
Distinctive Brutalist architecture designed by Ulrich Franzen Wikipedia
Two state-of-the-art stages: the Hubbard and Neuhaus Theatres City of Houston
History of premiering major works, including a "lost" Tennessee Williams play Alley Theatre
Extensive accessibility programs including ASL, captioning, and simultaneous Spanish translation Alley Theatre
attractions.tips: Arrive at least 30 minutes before the performance as the lobby opens one hour prior to curtain. Parking is available in the Theatre District Underground Parking (Green Level 1 for ADA) or the Alley Theatre Garage, which accepts credit cards only. For the best experience, use the digital ticket sharing feature to send tickets to your guests in advance Alley Theatre.
attractions.nearby: Jones Hall, Wortham Theater Center, Hobby Center for the Performing Arts, Market Square Park, Lyric Market Food Hall Alley Theatre

attractions.name: Arts District Houston (formerly Washington Avenue Arts District)
attractions.description: Arts District Houston is a state-appointed cultural district located along the Washington Avenue Corridor, boasting the highest concentration of working artists in Texas. The district encompasses the historic First and Sixth Wards and features a vibrant mix of over 300 artist studios, re-imagined industrial spaces, and iconic public murals. It serves as a hub for local creatives and offers a unique urban experience where visitors can see art in the making.
attractions.address: 2101 Winter Street, Houston, TX 77007 (Welcome Center at Winter Street Studios)
attractions.region: First Ward and Sixth Ward (Washington Avenue Corridor)
attractions.hours: Welcome Center: Saturday 12:00 PM – 5:00 PM, Wednesday – Friday by appointment; Office Hours: Tuesday – Thursday 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM; Art Alley at Sawyer Yards: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM Daily (Arts District Houston)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Outdoor public art is always accessible; specific venue hours vary)
attractions.admission: Free (Arts District Houston)
highlights: Sawyer Yards: A massive creative campus with over 300 artist studios and galleries.
Art Alley: An 800-foot-long collaborative mural featuring works from 14 different artists.
'Be Someone' Mural: An iconic, unofficial Houston landmark painted on a railroad bridge.
Historic Wards: Exploration of the First and Sixth Wards, two of Houston's oldest neighborhoods.
2nd Saturday Open Studios: A monthly event where artists open their workspaces to the public.
Market at Sawyer Yards: A curated monthly market featuring local makers and designers.
attractions.tips: The best time to visit is during '2nd Saturday Open Studios' to meet artists in person. There is free parking throughout the district, but the area is also highly bikeable with multiple BCycle stations available. (Arts District Houston)
attractions.nearby: Buffalo Bayou Park, Holler Brewing Co., Stanton's City Bites, Henderson & Kane General Store, Downtown Houston (Visit Houston)

attractions.name: Battleship Texas State Historic Site (USS Texas BB-35) (Battleship Texas Foundation)
attractions.description: The Battleship Texas is the world's last surviving dreadnought battleship and the only remaining capital ship to have served in both World War I and World War II. After a distinguished 34-year naval career, it became the first battleship memorial museum in the United States and is currently undergoing a massive $75 million restoration project to preserve its hull and interior for future generations (Battleship Texas Foundation, Wikipedia))
attractions.address: Pier 15, Galveston, TX 77550 (Permanent Home); previously 3523 Independence Pkwy S, La Porte, TX 77571 (Battleship Texas Foundation, Chron)
attractions.hours: Currently Closed for restoration; previous regular hours were 10:00 AM – 5:30 PM daily (Battleship Texas Foundation, Tripadvisor)
attractions.days_open: Currently Closed (Target reopening late 2026) (Battleship Texas Foundation)
attractions.admission: Currently Closed for Restoration; specialty "Hard Hat" or "Normandy" tours have been offered for $100 per person (Battleship Texas Foundation, FOX 26 Houston)
highlights: The only battleship in existence today that fought in both World War I and World War II (Battleship Texas Foundation)
First U.S. battleship to launch an aircraft from its deck in 1919 (Texas Historical Commission)
Provided critical naval gunfire support during the D-Day invasion at Normandy and the battles of Iwo Jima and Okinawa (Battleship Texas Foundation)
Designated as both a National Historic Landmark and a National Mechanical Engineering Landmark (Texas Historical Commission)
Recently relocated to Galveston's Pier 15 for permanent berthing following extensive hull repairs (Chron)
attractions.tips: While the ship is closed for restoration, visitors can get an excellent view of the vessel from the Pier 21 area in Galveston. Check the official website frequently for limited-availability 'Hard Hat' tours and the official reopening date expected in 2026 (Visit Galveston, Battleship Texas Foundation)
attractions.nearby: Galveston Historic Seaport (Tall Ship ELISSA), Ocean Star Offshore Drilling Rig & Museum, Galveston Naval Museum at Seawolf Park, and the San Jacinto Monument (Visit Galveston, Deer Park TX)

attractions.name: Bayou Bend Collection and Gardens
attractions.description: Bayou Bend is the Museum of Fine Arts, Houston's house museum for American decorative arts and paintings, situated on 14 acres of organically maintained gardens. Formerly the home of philanthropist Ima Hogg, it houses one of the world's finest collections of American furnishings, silver, and ceramics dating from 1620 to 1876 (MFAH, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Wednesday–Saturday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Sunday: 1:00 PM – 5:00 PM; Monday–Tuesday: Closed (except select holidays). Last admission is at 4:00 PM (MFAH).
attractions.admission: House + Gardens: $14 Adults, $12 Seniors (65+), Students (with ID), and Youth (13–18); Free for Children (12 & younger), MFAH Members, and Active Military. Gardens Only: $12 Adults, $10 Seniors, Students, and Youth; Free for Children (12 & younger) and MFAH Members. Free Family Days are held on the third Sunday of every month (MFAH, Texas Wanderers).
highlights: Hogg Family Legacy Room showcasing art and memorabilia of the philanthropic Hogg family (MFAH)
14 acres of formal and naturalistic gardens including eight distinct formal spaces (Wikipedia, MFAH)
The Lora Jean Kilroy Visitor and Education Center featuring an orientation gallery and library (MFAH)
Historic 1928 mansion designed by John F. Staub in the Latin Colonial style (Wikipedia)
Kitty King Powell Library and Study Center for art and decorative arts research (MFAH)
The Hogg Bridge, a suspension bridge over Buffalo Bayou providing a scenic entrance to the estate (Yelp)
attractions.tips: Park for free at the Lora Jean Kilroy Visitor Center and purchase tickets there first; the house is reached via a short walk and a suspension bridge over the bayou. Wear comfortable flat-heeled shoes for exploring the 14 acres of gardens and historic terrain. Reservations are recommended for guided tours, though self-guided options are available outside of tour times (MFAH, Yelp).
attractions.nearby: Memorial Park, Buffalo Bayou Park, Rienzi (MFAH European decorative arts house museum), Houston Arboretum & Nature Center, Beer Can House (Tripadvisor, MFAH).

attractions.name: Bayou City Community Market
attractions.description: Bayou City Community Market is a vibrant, women-owned and vendor-operated marketplace that serves as a hub for local farmers, artisans, and makers in Southwest Houston. The market is dedicated to providing fresh, locally grown produce and artisanal goods while fostering community connection through events, seasonal themes, and donation drives (Bayou City Community Market, DiningOut).
attractions.region: Westbury / Meyerland (Bayou City Community Market, Yelp)
attractions.hours: Saturday: 9:00 AM – 1:00 PM (2nd & 4th Saturdays only); Monday – Friday: Closed; Sunday: Closed (Bayou City Community Market)
attractions.days_open: Every 2nd and 4th Saturday of the month (Bayou City Community Market)
attractions.admission: Free (Bayou City Community Market, Eventbrite)
highlights: Texas-Certified producers-only farmers market featuring fresh in-season produce (Bayou City Community Market)
Diverse selection of ranch meats, eggs, local honey, and gourmet bakery items (Bayou City Community Market)
Regular community-focused events such as tree giveaways and donation drives (Eventbrite)
Features prepared foods like BBQ, Turkish pastries, and authentic Polish kielbasa (Eventbrite)
Lively atmosphere with a rotation of over 30 local vendors and artisans (Eventbrite)
attractions.tips: Arrive early for the best selection of fresh produce as popular items may sell out. Bring reusable bags or a backpack to carry your purchases, and check the online calendar for special community drives or seasonal giveaways (Bayou City Community Market, Bayou City Adventures).
attractions.nearby: Willow Waterhole Greenway, Westbury Square, Brays Bayou Greenway Trail, Meyerland Plaza, and the Nature Discovery Center (Reddit, Lemon Grove Realty)

attractions.name: Brays Bayou Greenway
attractions.description: Brays Bayou Greenway is a expansive 30-plus mile system of public greenspace and hike-and-bike trails that flows through the heart of southwestern Houston. It connects diverse neighborhoods, universities, and major landmarks like the Texas Medical Center and the University of Houston while providing scenic outdoor recreation.

Visitors can enjoy activities such as swimming, fishing, paddling through coastal trails, and exploring four miles of hiking paths. The park is a premier spot for bird watching, especially during spring and fall migrations, and provides both beach and bay-side recreational experiences (Texas Parks & Wildlife, Outdoorsy).

attractions.name: Moody Gardens
attractions.description: Moody Gardens is a top-rated family attraction and public, non-profit educational destination on Galveston Island, Texas. It features three iconic glass pyramids—the Aquarium, Rainforest, and Discovery pyramids—offering adventures in conservation, education, and research (Moody Gardens, Wikipedia).

attractions.name: Texas Southern University (Official Website)
attractions.description: Texas Southern University (TSU) is a public historically Black university (HBCU) located on a 150-acre campus in Houston's historic Third Ward. As one of the largest HBCUs in the nation, it offers over 100 academic programs and serves as a premier center for African American culture, excellence, and social justice. (TSU News, Official Website)
attractions.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Administrative Offices); Tuesday - Friday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Admin), 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM (University Museum); Saturday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM (University Museum); Sunday: Closed (University Museum, Official Website)
attractions.days_open: Monday through Saturday; Museum specifically open Tuesday through Saturday (University Museum, Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free for the University Museum and campus tours; parking is a $10 flat fee for visitors in designated lots (University Museum, Campus Parking)
highlights: One of the largest Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCUs) in the U.S. (TSU News)
Home to the University Museum, showcasing an extensive collection of African and African American art. (Texas Time Travel)
Houses the Barbara Jordan Papers archive and unique Hannah Hall student murals. (Texas Time Travel)
The only public university in Texas offering an Aviation Science Management degree with an on-campus flight simulator. (TSU News)
Notable alumni include U.S. Congresswoman Barbara Jordan and Grammy-winning artist Megan Thee Stallion. (Official Website)
attractions.tips: Schedule a guided tour with Tiger Ambassadors to explore the campus's rich traditions. Visitors should use the East Garage or Visitor Surface Lot for parking. Don't miss the Hannah Hall Murals and the historic Fairchild Building, which houses the University Museum. (Campus Tour Portal, University Museum)
attractions.nearby: Emancipation Park, University of Houston, Buffalo Soldiers National Museum, Houston Museum of African American Culture, and Project Row Houses (Jet Set Jazzmine, TSU News)

attractions.name: The Bronze Peacock at House of Blues Houston
attractions.description: The Bronze Peacock is an intimate, 2,211-square-foot music and event space located within the House of Blues Houston complex. The room pays homage to the legendary Bronze Peacock Ballroom, a historic African American-owned recording studio and ballroom in Houston. It features vibrant folk art, ornately painted walls, a private entrance, and a lighted stage, making it a popular choice for both live concerts and private corporate or social gatherings (Live Nation Special Events, Tagvenue).
attractions.hours: Show-dependent; typically 4:00 PM – 10:00 PM on event days; Box Office opens 1 hour before doors (Tagvenue, House of Blues Houston FAQ).
attractions.days_open: Tuesday through Sunday (typically show-dependent) (Houston Press, Tagvenue).
attractions.admission: Ticket prices vary by event, typically ranging from $25 to $65; some VIP or table packages can reach $150 or more per couple (Vivid Seats, Consequence Concerts).
attractions.phone: (888) 402-5837 (General Venue), (832) 667-7756 (Private Events) (House of Blues Houston Contact, Live Nation Special Events).
highlights: Intimate venue capacity of up to 200 guests for standing receptions (Tagvenue).
Features vibrant folk art and ornately painted walls that reflect a rich musical heritage (Live Nation Special Events).
Offers a private entrance on Dallas Street for direct access (Live Nation Special Events).
Equipped with its own lighted stage, private bar, and full-service production capabilities (Tagvenue).
Historical significance linked to Peacock Records and the original Bronze Peacock Ballroom (Blues Junction Productions).
attractions.tips: Arrive early to navigate downtown parking, which can range from $10-$20. Use the private entrance on Dallas Street for direct access to the Bronze Peacock room. The venue is cashless, so be sure to bring credit/debit or mobile payment options (Consequence Concerts, Live Nation Special Events).
attractions.nearby: Discovery Green, Toyota Center, George R. Brown Convention Center, Daikin Park (formerly Minute Maid Park), GreenStreet shopping and dining complex (Visit Houston).

attractions.name: The Galleria
attractions.description: The Galleria is Texas' largest and most luxurious shopping destination, featuring more than 400 stores and over 50 eateries across 2.4 million square feet (Official Website). This upscale mixed-use development also includes high-rise hotels, office towers, and a full-size year-round ice rink under a distinctive glass atrium (Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Monday - Thursday: 10:00 AM - 8:00 PM; Friday - Saturday: 10:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Sunday: 12:00 PM - 7:00 PM (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Official Website)
highlights: Texas' largest shopping mall and the 7th largest in the United States (Wikipedia)
Over 400 fine stores and restaurants, including luxury brands like Chanel and Louis Vuitton (Official Website)
Full-size year-round indoor ice rink for recreational and figure skating (Visit Houston)
Distinctive architectural design featuring a magnificent glass atrium (Official Website)
Anchored by major department stores including Neiman Marcus and Saks Fifth Avenue (Wikipedia)
attractions.tips: Visit during weekdays to avoid peak weekend crowds and wear comfortable shoes for the extensive walking required (Reddit). International visitors can utilize multiple tax-free reimbursement locations within the mall (Official Website).
attractions.nearby: Gerald D. Hines Waterwall Park, Williams Tower, Uptown Park, Highland Village, Memorial Park (Tripadvisor, Reddit)

attractions.name: The Original Ninfa's on Navigation
attractions.description: The Original Ninfa’s on Navigation is a historic Houston landmark and the birthplace of the modern fajita, where "Mama" Ninfa Laurenzo first served grilled skirt steak in handmade tortillas in 1973. It offers a casual yet elevated dining experience featuring a legendary blend of traditional Mexican and Tex-Mex cuisine. (Ninfas Official Website, Wikipedia)
attractions.region: Second Ward / East End (Yelp, Ninfas Official Website)
attractions.hours: Mon-Thu: 11 AM - 10 PM; Fri: 11 AM - 11 PM; Sat: 10 AM - 11 PM (Brunch 10 AM - 2 PM); Sun: 10 AM - 10 PM (Brunch 10 AM - 2 PM) (Ninfas Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Restaurant; menu prices apply) (Visit Houston)
highlights: Original Tacos al Carbon (Fajitas)
Fresh, handmade flour tortillas prepared on-site
The "Ninfarita" signature margarita
Historic 1973 original location in Houston's East End
Queso Flameado and creamy avocado "green sauce"
Weekend brunch featuring specialty Mexican breakfast items (Ninfas Official Website)
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended for dinner and weekends. The restaurant offers a free shuttle service to Astros games at Minute Maid Park starting two hours before game time. Don't miss the happy hour available Monday through Friday from 3 PM to 6 PM. (Ninfas Official Website, Family Review Guide)
attractions.nearby: Minute Maid Park, Toyota Center, Discovery Green, George R. Brown Convention Center, Houston East End Murals (Visit Houston, Family Review Guide)

attractions.name: The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center
attractions.description: The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center is the largest cancer center in the world and a global leader in cancer care, research, and education. Located in the Texas Medical Center, it consistently ranks as the top hospital for cancer care in the United States and is home to the most clinical trials in the world (Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Clinical Hours (Main Building): Mon-Fri 6 a.m.-8 p.m., Sat 7 a.m.-3 p.m., Sun 7 a.m.-7 p.m.; Visitation Hours: Mon-Fri 1-9 p.m., Weekends 10 a.m.-9 p.m. (MD Anderson)
attractions.admission: Free (Treatment and service costs vary) (MD Anderson)
highlights: Consistently ranked #1 for cancer care in the U.S. by U.S. News & World Report (Wikipedia)
Largest cancer center globally, providing care to over 1.2 million individuals since 1944 (Wikipedia)
Home to the highest number of cancer clinical trials in the world (Wikipedia)
Includes the James P. Allison Institute, dedicated to advancing cancer immunotherapy (Wikipedia)
Features the Children's Art Project, which supports pediatric cancer patients through art sales (Wikipedia)
Part of the world-renowned Texas Medical Center campus (Wikipedia)
attractions.tips: Download the MD Anderson Mobile app for wayfinding and appointment tracking. Check your specific appointment building address as the campus is vast and spread across multiple locations. Parking is available at various TMC garages, and valet services are offered at main entrances (MD Anderson).
attractions.nearby: Hermann Park, Houston Zoo, Museum of Fine Arts Houston, Houston Museum of Natural Science, Rice University (TripAdvisor, Reddit)

attractions.name: Theodore Rex (Theodore Rex)
attractions.description: Theodore Rex is a relaxed fine-dining restaurant featuring modern American cuisine with a French touch and a focus on local ingredients (Theodore Rex). Led by James Beard Award-winning chef Justin Yu, the establishment offers a personal and playful culinary experience in a converted warehouse space (Thorough Fare).
attractions.hours: Monday: 5:00 PM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: Closed, Wednesday: Closed, Thursday: 5:00 PM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 5:00 PM – 10:00 PM, Saturday: 5:00 PM – 10:00 PM, Sunday: 5:00 PM – 10:00 PM (Theodore Rex)
attractions.days_open: Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday, Monday (Theodore Rex)
attractions.admission: A la carte menu; private family-style menu is $95 per person (Theodore Rex Events Brochure). Mains are typically priced around $30 (Reddit).
highlights: Famous for its signature "Tomato Toast" appetizer (Yelp)
Located in a historic building in Houston's Warehouse District (Theodore Rex)
Emphasis on seasonal and locally sourced ingredients (Theodore Rex)
Casual yet sophisticated atmosphere suitable for special occasions (Theodore Rex)
Extensive and thoughtfully curated wine list (Theodore Rex)
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended through Resy, though the bar and select tables are available for walk-in guests (Houston Food Finder). Free off-street and street parking are typically available in the surrounding area (Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: Saint Arnold Brewing Company, Dean's Downtown, McIntyre's, Market Square Park (Downtown Houston)

attractions.name: Topgolf Houston - Katy
attractions.description: Topgolf Houston - Katy is a premier entertainment destination featuring climate-controlled hitting bays, a high-tech golf game, and an outstanding food and beverage menu. It offers a unique social experience for both golfers and non-golfers, blending technology and entertainment in a multi-story complex. Topgolf
attractions.hours: Mon-Sun: 9:00 AM - 1:00 AM Topgolf Houston-Katy
attractions.admission: Monday-Thursday: $20-$39/hr; Friday: $20-$62/hr; Saturday: $20-$66/hr; Sunday: $20-$45/hr (prices are per bay for up to 6 players). Half Price Tuesday: 50% off game play all day. New players require a $6 account activation fee. Topgolf Houston-Katy
highlights: 100+ climate-controlled hitting bays across 3 floors
Toptracer technology for high-tech automatic scoring
Full-service restaurant and multiple sports bars
Rooftop terrace with fire pits and outdoor seating
Over 200 HDTVs and complimentary Wi-Fi throughout the venue
Complimentary golf club rentals for all ages and skill levels
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended to avoid long wait times, especially on weekends and peak hours. Visit on Tuesdays for half-price game play all day. Guests under 16 must be supervised by someone 21+ at all times, and those under 18 must be supervised after 9:00 PM. Topgolf Experience Guide
attractions.nearby: Bear Creek Pioneers Park, CityCentre (Shopping & Dining), Memorial City Mall, Terry Hershey Park Hike & Bike Trails Tripadvisor, Secret Houston

attractions.name: Underbelly Hospitality, LLC
attractions.description: Underbelly Hospitality is an acclaimed Houston-based restaurant group founded in 2012 by James Beard Award-winning chef Chris Shepherd. The group is dedicated to creating community spaces that celebrate local farmers and vendors through diverse culinary concepts ranging from fine dining steakhouses to casual burger shops. Currently, the company operates several prominent Houston establishments including Georgia James, Underbelly Burger, and Pastore Italian Kitchen.
attractions.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Thursday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Saturday: Closed; Sunday: Closed (Corporate Office Hours)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Corporate Office); Restaurant hours vary by location (7 days a week)
attractions.admission: Free (Restaurant prices vary by location)
highlights: Operates diverse restaurant concepts including Georgia James, Underbelly Burger, Wild Oats, and Pastore Italian Kitchen.
Strong focus on sourcing from local farmers and vendors, including 44 Farms and R-C Ranch.
Georgia James features a signature rooftop terrace overlooking the city.
Includes concepts located within the historic Houston Farmers Market.
Recipient of numerous culinary accolades, reflecting Houston's diverse culture.
attractions.tips: Advance reservations are highly recommended for flagship locations like Georgia James. High school students can receive a 15% discount on burger combos at the Witte Road location of Underbelly Burger with a valid ID. Visit the Houston Farmers Market to experience multiple group concepts and local vendors in a single outing.
attractions.nearby: Buffalo Bayou Park, Houston Farmers Market, Memorial Park, The Heights neighborhood

attractions.name: University of Houston
attractions.description: The University of Houston is a major public research institution and the flagship of the University of Houston System (Wikipedia). Founded in 1927, it serves nearly 47,000 students and is recognized for its high research activity and diverse academic programs (The Princeton Review).
attractions.address: 4800 Calhoun Road, Houston, TX 77004 (University of Houston)
attractions.hours: Student Centers: Mon–Thu 7:00 am – 12:00 am, Fri–Sat 7:00 am – 1:00 am, Sun 1:00 pm – 12:00 am; Welcome Center: Mon–Fri 8:30 am – 5:00 pm (Student Centers, Welcome Center)
attractions.admission: Free for general campus access; tours are free but require advance registration (Undergraduate Admissions)
highlights: Tier One public research institution offering over 300 degree programs (QS World University Rankings)
Home to the TDECU Stadium and Fertitta Center, supporting a historic Division I athletics program (Wikipedia)
Extensive public art collection and the landmark M.D. Anderson Library (University of Houston)
Centrally located campus with pedestrian-friendly paths and the iconic Cullen Family Plaza reflecting pool (University of Houston Stories)
Student Center features a wide range of amenities including dining, a bowling alley, and a bookstore (University of Houston YouTube)
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to park in the Welcome Center Garage or East Garage using the pay-by-phone system (University of Houston YouTube). Wear comfortable walking shoes as the campus spans over 600 acres, and check the interactive map at uh.edu/maps before your arrival (Undergraduate Admissions).
attractions.nearby: Houston Museum District, Hermann Park, Houston Zoo, Discovery Green, and the Texas Medical Center (Tripadvisor, Reddit)

attractions.name: Upper Kirby Management District
attractions.description: Upper Kirby is a vibrant commercial district in Houston known for its blend of historic neighborhoods and modern urban development. The area is distinguished by its British-themed streetscape, including iconic red phone booths, and serves as a premier destination for upscale dining, shopping, and community events. (Visit Houston, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Tuesday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Wednesday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Thursday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Friday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Saturday: Closed, Sunday: Closed (District office hours; public areas open 24/7) (Wikipedia, Prospeo)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (District office); Daily (District public areas) (Wikipedia)
attractions.admission: Free (Wikipedia)
highlights: Iconic red British-style phone booths and street signs
Levy Park - an award-winning urban park with a performance pavilion and dog park
Historic Alabama Theatre - a 1930s landmark now housing a unique Trader Joe's
West Ave - a major mixed-use development featuring boutiques and fine dining
River Oaks Garden Club Forum of Civics - listed on the National Register of Historic Places
Celebrated local dining landmarks like Tony's and Kirby Ice House
attractions.tips: Be sure to look for the various red phone booths throughout the district for iconic photo opportunities. Visit Levy Park for free community programs like outdoor yoga and concerts. Many restaurants in the area offer lively happy hours popular with the local workforce. (It's Not Hou It's Me, Levy Park)
attractions.nearby: Greenway Plaza, River Oaks, Lakewood Church, The Galleria, Museum District (Tripadvisor, Visit Houston)

attractions.name: Warehouse District
attractions.description: The Warehouse District in Houston is a vibrant and historic urban enclave situated on the northeast edge of downtown, known for its eclectic mix of industrial architecture and thriving creative community. Once a bustling hub of commerce along the Buffalo Bayou, it has evolved into one of the country's oldest urban art colonies, housing numerous artist studios, galleries, and award-winning restaurants. Visitors can explore a unique landscape where historic warehouses have been transformed into modern lofts, creative workspaces, and lively entertainment venues.
attractions.hours: District: Open 24/7; Open Studios: Third Saturday of each month 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM; Apartment Office: Mon-Fri 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM, Sat 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Sun 1:00 PM - 5:00 PM
attractions.days_open: Daily (24/7 for the district; specific hours for events and businesses)
attractions.admission: Free (for public areas and recurring Open Studio events)
highlights: Home to one of the oldest urban art colonies in the country, featuring historic industrial buildings repurposed into creative spaces.
Hosts "Third Saturday Open Studios" and the annual "Art Crawl," allowing visitors to interact with local artists and purchase original works.
Features a diverse array of working artist studios, including Hardy & Nance Studios and Mother Dog Studios.
Includes award-winning dining destinations such as Theodore Rex and historic music venues like Last Concert Cafe.
Offers unique residential loft living and "Essential Workforce Housing" through the Warehouse District development.
Located near the Buffalo Bayou, providing a mix of urban grit and natural beauty.
attractions.tips: Visit during "Third Saturday Open Studios" (12:00 PM – 5:00 PM) for the best experience interacting with artists and viewing their latest works. The area is highly walkable, so wearing comfortable shoes is recommended for hopping between galleries and nearby restaurants like Theodore Rex. Parking is generally available on the street or in designated lots for events, often for free. For a deep dive into the local art scene, time your visit for the annual Art Crawl held in November.
attractions.nearby: Buffalo Bayou Park, Saint Arnold Brewing Company, Minute Maid Park, Theodore Rex, Last Concert Cafe, Downtown Houston Aquarium
